rfcs.title: RFC 1945 — HTTP/1.0
rfcs.description: Hypertext Transfer Protocol version 1.0. Defines basic request/response message format, method semantics (GET, HEAD, POST), status codes, and simple entity body boundaries via Content-Length or connection close. No persistent connections, no chunked encoding, one request per connection. TurboHttp compliance: 85/100.

rfcs.title: RFC 6265 — HTTP State Management (Cookies)
rfcs.description: HTTP cookie mechanism for state management. Defines Set-Cookie/Cookie headers, domain and path matching, cookie attributes (Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite, Max-Age, Expires), and storage model. MUST requirements: domain matching rules, path matching, Secure attribute enforcement, cookie ordering. TurboHttp compliance: 80/100.

rfcs.title: RFC 7541 — HPACK: Header Compression for HTTP/2
rfcs.description: HPACK header compression format for HTTP/2. Defines static table (61 entries), dynamic table with FIFO eviction, indexed/literal header representations, Huffman encoding, and table size management. MUST requirements: dynamic table synchronization, sensitive header never-indexed treatment, table size bounds. TurboHttp compliance: 90/100.

rfcs.title: RFC 9000 — QUIC: A UDP-Based Multiplexed and Secure Transport
rfcs.description: QUIC transport protocol over UDP with built-in TLS 1.3. Defines connection establishment, stream multiplexing, flow control, loss detection, congestion control, and connection migration. TurboHttp implements only variable-length integer encoding and basic packet header parsing. TurboHttp compliance: 50/100 (partial — primitives only).

rfcs.title: RFC 9110 — HTTP Semantics
rfcs.description: Core HTTP semantics shared by all versions. Defines methods (GET, HEAD, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH), status codes, content negotiation, conditional requests, redirects (301/302/303/307/308), idempotent retry logic, and authentication framework. MUST requirements: method safety, redirect method rewriting, Retry-After handling, content-length consistency. TurboHttp compliance: 82/100.

rfcs.title: RFC 9111 — HTTP Caching
rfcs.description: HTTP caching model for shared and private caches. Defines freshness lifetime calculation (max-age, s-maxage, Expires, heuristic), validation via conditional requests (304 Not Modified), Cache-Control directives, Vary-based secondary keys, and cache storage/retrieval. MUST requirements: no-store compliance, stale response handling, validation request construction. TurboHttp compliance: 90.6/100 (79/116 requirements implemented).

rfcs.title: RFC 9112 — HTTP/1.1
rfcs.description: HTTP/1.1 message syntax and connection management. Defines request-line format, required Host header, chunked transfer coding, Content-Length framing, persistent connections (keep-alive/close), pipelining, and message body boundaries. MUST requirements: Host header presence, Content-Length/Transfer-Encoding consistency, chunked decoding, connection close semantics. TurboHttp compliance: 92/100.

rfcs.title: RFC 9113 — HTTP/2
rfcs.description: HTTP/2 binary framing protocol. Defines 9-byte frame header, frame types (DATA, HEADERS, CONTINUATION, SETTINGS, PING, GOAWAY, WINDOW_UPDATE, RST_STREAM, PUSH_PROMISE), stream state machine, multiplexing, flow control, HPACK header compression, and connection preface. MUST requirements: connection preface validation, SETTINGS acknowledgment, flow control enforcement, pseudo-header ordering. TurboHttp compliance: 87/100.

rfcs.title: RFC 9114 — HTTP/3
rfcs.description: HTTP/3 protocol over QUIC transport. Defines variable-length frame headers, frame types (DATA, HEADERS, CANCEL_PUSH, SETTINGS, PUSH_PROMISE, GOAWAY, MAX_PUSH_ID), unidirectional stream types (control, push, encoder, decoder), QPACK header compression integration, and connection shutdown. TurboHttp compliance: 60/100 (partial — frame parsing and stream types implemented, advanced features missing).

rfcs.title: RFC 9204 — QPACK: Field Compression for HTTP/3
rfcs.description: QPACK header compression for HTTP/3, designed to avoid head-of-line blocking. Defines static table, dynamic table with streamed updates, encoder/decoder instruction streams, blocking references, and section acknowledgment. Uses QUIC variable-length integers for indices. TurboHttp compliance: 40/100 (draft — decoder implemented, encoder and instruction processing missing).
